selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":88,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"bisexual","occupation":"not applicable","city_of_living":"Washington DC","employment_status":"retired","education_level":"vocational diploma","parents_education":{"parent_1":"no formal education","parent_2":"master's degree"},"current_marital_status":"in a relationship","children":2,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"normal","religious_beliefs":"Hinduism","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media","hobbies":["knitting","photography","cycling"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":96,"level":"high"},"conscientiousness":{"score":89,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":45,"level":"moderate"},"agreeableness":{"score":73,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":20,"level":"low"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

mode = human
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = {"age":88,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"bisexual","occupation":"not applicable","city_of_living":"Washington DC","employment_status":"retired","education_level":"vocational diploma","parents_education":{"parent_1":"no formal education","parent_2":"master's degree"},"current_marital_status":"in a relationship","children":2,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"normal","religious_beliefs":"Hinduism","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media","hobbies":["knitting","photography","cycling"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":96,"level":"high"},"conscientiousness":{"score":89,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":45,"level":"moderate"},"agreeableness":{"score":73,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":20,"level":"low"}}}

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "As an individual who has spent much of his life navigating the world, I believe that managing fake content on social media is a matter of education and discernment. Having grown up before television was ubiquitous, I remember when information had fewer filters and required more critical thinking from people to verify its authenticity. Now, with digital platforms like these, it's similar but amplified by speed and reach. My own approach involves reminding myself that not everything presented online is reliable. I've learned to cross-reference sources whenever possible and to be wary of content that seems sensational or too good to be true.",
  "reasoning_summary": "Firstly, drawing from my background as someone who experienced pre-digital information dissemination, I understand the importance of skepticism. Secondly, being retired with a moderate amount of time on social media each week allows me to balance exposure without overwhelming myself. Thirdly, my high agreeableness makes me appreciate content that fosters harmony but also requires maintaining truthful discourse. Fourthly, despite high conscientiousness in other areas, my low extraversion means I don't seek out every piece of information; instead, I focus on what resonates or seems credible.",
  "tone": "Calm, reflective and informative",
  "citations": ["Personal experience from analog media consumption"],
  "safety_notes": "None",
  "used_persona_fields":["age", "gender", "time_onsocialmedia", "hobbies", "ocean.extro", "ocean.agreeableness"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As an individual who has spent much of his life navigating the world, I believe that managing fake content on social media is a matter of education and discernment. Having grown up before television was ubiquitous, I remember when information had fewer filters and required more critical thinking from people to verify its authenticity. Now, with digital platforms like these, it's similar but amplified by speed and reach. My own approach involves reminding myself that not everything presented online is reliable. I've learned to cross-reference sources whenever possible and to be wary of content that seems sensational or too good to be true.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Firstly, drawing from my background as someone who experienced pre-digital information dissemination, I understand the importance of skepticism. Secondly, being retired with a moderate amount of time on social media each week allows me to balance exposure without overwhelming myself. Thirdly, my high agreeableness makes me appreciate content that fosters harmony but also requires maintaining truthful discourse. Fourthly, despite high conscientiousness in other areas, my low extraversion means I don't seek out every piece of information; instead, I focus on what resonates or seems credible.
citations: Personal experience from analog media consumption
